item.title: Bumper season brings cheaper avocados, 'record' exports, but growers pay price
item.description: A bumper avocado season is creating a perfect storm for cheaper produce and record export volumes, but growers are doing it tough.

item.title: How a youth outreach program is keeping teens engaged in Kalumburu
item.description: Kalumburu youth workers are giving teens the chance to get away from screens, be on country and connect with nature.

item.title: Australia to join 34 countries — but not the US — in meeting on Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Australia will join a virtual meeting of 35 countries to discuss plans to reopen and secure the Strait of Hormuz, and ease the oil shock rocking global markets.

item.title: Carlton's poor second halves are the talk of the AFL, so what is going wrong for the Blues?
item.description: Carlton's horrible second halves have become a costly trend which has the team struggling and Michael Voss fighting for his job. This is what is going wrong after half-time for the Blues.

item.title: Police decision to call off ambulance for Jeffrey Winmar scrutinised during custodial death inquest
item.description: The events leading up to the death of Indigenous father Jeffrey Winmar in police custody in Melbourne's east in 2023 are being examined through a coronial inquest.

item.title: Matildas' players join Japan criticising 'inequality' in prize money from Asian Football Confederation
item.description: Fierce rivals on the field, the Matildas and Japan have joined forces to slam the Asian Football Confederation's prize money for the Women's Asian Cup.

item.title: Cate Blanchett reunites with Jim Jarmusch on anthology movie Father Mother Sister Brother
item.description: The complexities of family light up Jim Jarmusch's Golden Lion-winner, Father Mother Sister Brother, which features Blanchett alongside Adam Driver and Tom Waits.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: A lost penguin, successful NASA rocket launch and address to the nation
item.description: It's been a big week in the news. Did you catch these stories?

item.title: Australia sources most of its jet fuel from Asia, but suppliers are feeling the pinch
item.description: Australia imports most of its jet fuel from Asia, particularly China, Singapore and South Korea. But those countries rely on the Middle East for crude oil, meaning they are particularly vulnerable to the impacts of war.

item.title: South Australian MP Jason Virgo's switch from the Australian Sex Party to One Nation
item.description: Sixteen years before Jason Virgo won the SA seat of MacKillop for One Nation in the state's south-east, he twice ran for parliament as an Australian Sex Party candidate.

item.title: What lowering the diesel flash point means for Australia's refineries
item.description: The Australian government has lowered the diesel flashpoint for the next six months by one degree Celsius — but what does that actually mean?

item.title: Fuel crisis driving some Tasmanian farmers to consider abandoning harvests
item.description: Tasmanian farmers are considering not harvesting crops like broccoli and cauliflower because increasing freight costs are proving to be so debilitating.

item.title: Matildas captain Sam Kerr shoots down Chelsea exit rumour after report of potential US move
item.description: Matildas captain Sam Kerr is quick to cast doubt on a report she will leave Chelsea at the end of the season to join US club Denver Summit.

item.title: Cameron Green's IPL woes for Knight Riders continue as Travis Head fires for Sunrisers
item.description: Cameron Green is unable to arrest his shaky start to the Indian Premier League season, but Travis Head blasts back to his best.

item.title: Cafe owners calls for visa changes to stop backpackers leaving WA's holiday hotspots
item.description: Visa rules allow backpackers to conduct "remote work" on the island, just 33km from Perth's city centre, but not in WA's South West — something business owners want to change.

item.title: Port Macquarie family travels overseas for unproven stem-cell treatment as experts urge caution
item.description: A young mother who travelled overseas for controversial stem cell therapy hopes new treatments will one day be available in Australia, but experts warn there's still a "long way" to go.

item.title: Australia scores nine-wicket win over West Indies in final ODI in St Kitts
item.description: Alana King stars with the ball, as the Australian women's team finish their white-ball tour of West Indies with a resounding nine-wicket win in St Kitts.

item.title: Fitzroy River plan sparks concern among pastoralists and traditional owners
item.description: A long-awaited draft plan aimed at protecting one of Western Australia's largest rivers has been released for public consultation, but traditional owners, environmental groups and pastoralists say key changes are needed.

item.title: SA Electoral Commission apologises to staff over pay delay following state election
item.description: The Electoral Commission of South Australia has apologised to staff who have not been fully paid almost a fortnight after the state election, saying a payroll processing delay had caused the problem.

item.title: NASA Artemis II astronauts rocket towards Moon after spending day orbiting Earth
item.description: The Orion capsule carrying four astronauts in NASA's Artemis II mission successfully fired its engines this morning, kicking the crew out of Earth's orbit and bound for the Moon.

item.title: Westpac CEO warns 'there's a chance of a recession' as interest rates rise
item.description: Westpac's chief executive, Anthony Miller, says Australians need to acknowledge there is a growing risk the country could slip into recession, amid the Iran war-driven fuel crisis and rising interest rates.

item.title: World loses patience with Trump as war no longer just about Iran
item.description: Global leaders are giving up the pretence of diplomatic niceties as they lose patience with the US president's actions.

item.title: Best podcasts to listen to this Easter long weekend, from Expanse: The Nannup Four to Empire City
item.description: The podcasts we're listening to this weekend include an investigation into the Salt Path scandal and a personal look at the role of Australia in the end of apartheid.

item.title: One Nation leaves SA Liberals clinging to opposition status
item.description: The Liberal Party will have just one more seat than One Nation in South Australia’s lower house, leaving opposition leader Ashton Hurn little margin for error.

item.title: Australian grey-headed flying fox economic and ecological value of poo revealed
item.description: Bats are often thought of as smelly, ugly and noisy, but a study has revealed the economic and ecological value of one Australian species's poo.

item.title: Avocado 'ripeness scanner' trial aims to help customers choose the perfect fruit
item.description: An Australian-first avocado "ripeness scanner" is being trialled, with the aim of telling customers exactly when their avocados will be ready to eat.

item.title: Art created to help Papunya children read in their language goes on display at National Library of Australia
item.description: More than 40 years ago, a program was created to help Papunya children in remote NT learn to read in Pintupi-Luritja languages. Those books are now an insight into the people, stories, history and culture of the Papunya and are on display at the National Library of Australia.

item.title: Why 18-year-old Daniel Williams is among the rising stars of Australian athletics
item.description: Teenagers Gout Gout and Cameron Myers are already creating an impact on the international stage, helping to inspire the likes of 18-year-old Daniel Williams to follow in their footsteps.

item.title: Interest in novated leases for electric vehicles soaring in Australia amid fuel crisis
item.description: With the petrol bowser continuing to hurt wallets, more Australians than ever are considering getting an electric vehicle through a novated lease.

item.title: How often should you change your toilet brush?
item.description: If you're just shoving the brush into its holder to wait for the next use and actively avoiding thinking about how dirty the whole concept of it is, you're not alone. Here's how to make sure your toilet brush is as hygienic as possible while it lasts.

item.title: Farmer and First Nations artist collaborate to create beautiful, cultural fashion
item.description: When a farmer and a First Nations artist join forces, something beautiful emerges — thanks to Japanese knitting technology.

item.title: Local governments support move to add world-famous Bibbulmun Track to heritage list
item.description: Councils support moves for the Bibbulmun Track to be declared a heritage-listed site, which conservationists hope could protect the track from future mining expansions.

item.title: A quick dinner with butter beans, leeks and spinach - ABC News
item.description: This quick and easy butter bean recipe from Helen Tzouganatos might solve your dinner woes.

item.title: Woman charged with murder following investigation into death of 24yo in Laverton
item.description: The woman, 48, and the deceased were known to each other, according to Homicide Squad detectives.

item.title: Man charged with murder after missing woman's body was discovered in Little River
item.description: A 67-year-old man has been charged with murder after the body of a woman who had been missing since Thursday was discovered south-west of Melbourne.

item.title: Hard Quiz: Hurry up, I don't have all day to watch you struggle at internet trivia
item.description: It's painfully obvious you're going to fail this quiz, so let's get it over with and return to our Sunday mornings, shall we?

item.title: Sam Williamson puts sickening knee injury behind him to target 2026 Commonwealth Games
item.description: A career-threatening knee injury denied swimmer Sam Williamson the chance to defend his world title. Now he's back and looking to replicate the form that took him to the top of the world, by earning a place at the Commonwealth Games.

item.title: New research shows regional Australians move on foot more in 'walkable' towns
item.description: The humble footpath could be the answer to being more active, with new research showing regional Australians living in "walkable towns" travelled 75 minutes more on foot each week.

item.title: Aerial photographer's hunt for salt lake 'Easter eggs' yields striking images in WA
item.description: Scott McCook spends his spare time photographing the salt lakes of WA's Wheatbelt, which look uncannily like giant eggs when viewed from the air.

item.title: Artemis astronauts preparing for historic lunar flyby
item.description: The Artemis astronauts have been taking a moment to look back at Earth as they continue deep into space toward the Moon.

item.title: 'Changing genders wasn't going to change how I felt about Kate'
item.description: Kate could see her husband was miserable as a man and says she couldn't watch Marti be unhappy.

item.title: Lily Mora explores origins of Australian modernism in new show at Heide Museum of Modern Art
item.description: A new show at the Heide Museum of Modern Art explores how the famous Heide Circle reimagined the family in radical ways in the 50s and 60s.

item.title: Australia given fuel supply assurances as plans in works for visit by Japan's prime minister
item.description: Fuel will continue to flow from Japan into Australia under assurances given during recent talks between the two countries.

item.title: This week in history quiz, April 6: Take a walk down memory lane
item.description: Where was the Venus de Milo found? And what common household item first went on sale in April 1827?

item.title: As Iran charges for access to Strait of Hormuz, world faces dangerous new era of maritime tollbooths
item.description: If Iran's idea of charging ships to pass through maritime choke points catches on, we are entering a new era of much more expensive, more dangerous global shipping.

item.title: Cape Grim's 50-year air record reveals true scale of humans' impact on planet
item.description: Perched on Tasmania's wild north-west coast, Cape Grim has been quietly tracking the world's cleanest air for 50 years. Its data tell an interesting story about how our world has changed over half a century, including rare success stories.

item.title: Terminal diagnosis hasn't stopped the final exhibition for Joan Humble
item.description: After a lifetime of capturing Tasmanian landscapes, Joan Humble is holding her final exhibition. The 88-year-old has a terminal cancer diagnosis, but she is determined to keep painting as long as she can hold a brush.

item.title: Geelong's Corio Bowls skate park earmarked for heritage protection
item.description: A set of skate bowls with origins as an outlet for teens in a tough Geelong suburb, once visited by Tony Hawk, may soon be preserved long-term through heritage protection.

item.title: Feral animals, biosecurity and careers on agenda for SA Aboriginal Land and Sea Rangers
item.description: The first SA Aboriginal Land and Sea Ranger Conference has brought together those on the front line of the fight against feral animals and plants.

item.title: Sydney Royal Easter Show helps emerging artists build their careers
item.description: Horse drawings and handcrafted ceramics from local artists and artisans are among the arts and crafts you can find at the Sydney Royal Easter Show.

item.title: Artist shares origin story of Field of Light installation at Uluru
item.description: Bruce Munro's art installation is inspired by Uluru. Ten years later, it remains one of the most popular attractions at the famous landmark.

item.title: Artemis II crew about to smash record for the farthest humans have ventured from Earth
item.description: The Apollo 13 crew has held the distance record for a crewed aircraft's maximum range from Earth since the 1970s. The Artemis II crew is about to change that.

item.title: How US forces pulled off daring rescue of stranded airman shot down in Iran
item.description: After an American fighter jet was shot down in a mountainous area of Iran, the US military pulled off an incredible rescue of two airmen, living up to its credo of "no man left behind", despite running into difficulty.

item.title: Three Italian-inspired sausage recipes
item.description: If you want some new ways to use sausages, these three easy Italian-inspired recipes will get you excited about them.

item.title: Marshall questions penalty against Tigers despite Eels star's ugly ankle injury
item.description: As the Eels grapple with another potentially season-ending injury to Bailey Simonsson, Wests Tigers coach Benji Marshall says he is not even sure the tackle that led to it was worth a penalty.

item.title: Michael Maguire faces first as Broncos coach amid injury crisis
item.description: Brisbane coach Michael Maguire is facing a brand new selection challenge against North Queensland with Ben Hunt, Adam Reynolds and Reece Walsh set to be absent at the same time.

item.title: Byron bounces back after Bluesfest cancellation as Easter crowds enjoy musical treats
item.description: There was no Bluesfest, but thousands of music fans still packed venues across the Byron Shire over the Easter long weekend.

item.title: Mollie O'Callaghan, Kyle Chalmers hail depth, Sam Short shows class on night one of Australian Open swimming
item.description: Mollie O'Callaghan and Kyle Chalmers claimed Australian Open gold medals in the 100m freestyle events, while Sam Short showed his 400m rivals a clean pair of heels on night one at the Gold Coast Aquatic Centre.

item.title: Views from the Artemis II mission are absolutely 'unreal', astronaut says — as it happened
item.description: The Artemis II astronauts have travelled further from Earth than any humans before, completing a historic fly-by of the Moon.

item.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?
item.description: Today's ABC Places quiz features photos from 10 locations around the world. Your challenge is to guess where you are.

item.title: Farrer candidate funded by Rupert Murdoch's pro-climate son-in-law
item.description: Backers of Farrer independent candidate Michelle Milthorpe are celebrating after securing the support of a prominent pro-climate farmer who is also the son-in-law of media mogul Rupert Murdoch.

item.title: Zein is first-generation Syrian Australian, a title that carries the weight of two worlds
item.description: For Heywire winner Zein, her family is remembered in the fragments she remembers of Syria.

item.title: AACo spared investigation into cattle thirst deaths after reassuring regulators
item.description: The lack of investigation into deaths at the $700 million beef business has been described as "completely unacceptable" by a legal expert who argued industry had captured government regulators.

item.title: SA Voice members say some First Nations voters felt 'judged racially' by electoral staff
item.description: South Australia's Aboriginal Affairs Minister will consider an independent review of the state's Voice to Parliament election, following claims some voters were queried about their racial background at polling booths, while others were forced to queue for hours.

item.title: Man charged with murder over stabbing at Buff Point on NSW Central Coast
item.description: A man remains in custody after facing a bail court hearing after being charged with murder over a stabbing at Buff Point, on the NSW Central Coast.

item.title: Canberra paraplegic woman stranded in hospital for 19 months due to aged care bed block
item.description: After she was stuck in a Canberra hospital for 19 months, paraplegic 84-year-old Mary Wansey has lost hope of ever finding a place in aged care.

item.title: WA man considering legal action after being sent home from hospital when he needed surgery
item.description: An Australian Navy candidate says he nearly lost his leg following delays in diagnosis and treatment of his emergency condition by two Perth hospitals.

item.title: PVC prices soar amid petrol and gas supply issues as Iran war continues
item.description: Conflict in the Middle East has disrupted petroleum and gas supply chains, causing PVC prices to skyrocket in Australia.

item.title: Queensland maritime authorities criticised for 'slow' wrecked boats salvage
item.description: The vessel 7 Wonder has sat wrecked at Rose Bay in north Queensland for more than two months. Locals say they have been picking up rubbish from the boat daily, but maritime authorities say local salvage contractors are "stretched".

item.title: Fears for Ningaloo Reef after ex-cyclone decimates region amid coral spawning season
item.description: As sharks swarm in the aftermath, there are fears ex-tropical Cyclone Narelle may have triggered a climate disaster for the world-famous Ningaloo Reef.

item.title: Don't ask me about my love life. I'm busy writing a different story
item.description: I might be on a different path, but I can still love and be loved in ways that don't require me to be a mother or a partner.

item.title: 'Vegan leather' isn't as sustainable or eco‑friendly as brands might claim
item.description: As fashion brands move away from animal leather, "vegan" has come to suggest something that is both kinder to animals and better for the planet. However, the reality is more complicated.

item.title: Brisbane Broncos name Reece Walsh replacement, Victor Radley returns from suspension
item.description: Jesse Arthars has been named at fullback for the Brisbane Broncos ahead of Friday's blockbuster clash against the North Queensland Cowboys.

item.title: Australian War Memorial to review Ben Roberts-Smith display following war crimes charges
item.description: The Australian War Memorial will review its display of Ben Roberts-Smith, who has been charged with committing war crimes.

item.title: Maree Mavis Crabtree pleads not guilty to murdering son Jonathan with spiked fruit smoothie
item.description: Maree Mavis Crabtree has pleaded not guilty to the murder of her son Jonathan in 2017, his attempted murder earlier that year, and attempted fraud.

item.title: Kaylee McKeown, Lani Pallister and Kyle Chalmers claim wins at Australian Open swimming championships
item.description: Bugs swarm the Gold Coast Aquatic Centre pool as Australia's best and brightest swimming talents showed their versatility on day two of the Australian Open championships.

item.title: Travel quiz, April 8: Pack your suitcase, we're going on a trip
item.description: Which European microstate is smaller? And which US president isn’t on Mount Rushmore?

item.title: Sword of the Sea is the Grammy Award-winning follow-up from the team behind Journey
item.description: Art director Matt Nava and composer Austin Wintory reveal how their cult 2012 video game Journey shaped their latest offering, the Grammy Award-winning Sword of the Sea.

item.title: The grief of being estranged from family
item.description: Estrangement usually begins after a significant traumatic experience with a loved one, experts say.

item.title: PM urged to intervene in youth justice systems to break the cycle of crime
item.description: Hundreds of legal, health and children's organisations are calling on Anthony Albanese to convene an emergency summit to address the over-incarceration of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander children.

item.title: National park expansion fuels land price surge and anxiety of NSW farmers
item.description: Hailed as a win for the environment, $200 million has been spent purchasing new national parks in outback NSW — but not everyone is celebrating.

item.title: Alex de Minaur defeats Cameron Norrie at Monte Carlo Masters round of 32
item.description: Alex de Minaur is through to the final 16 of the Monte-Carlo Masters after defeating Britain's Cameron Norrie.

item.title: Queensland premier calls for fast-track of oil projects amid fuel crisis
item.description: Queensland Premier David Crisafulli wants the federal government to fast-track environmental approvals for oil projects.

item.title: Squid sightings spawn hope in SA amid calls for longer-lasting restrictions
item.description: Sightings of southern calamari in South Australia are bringing relief after the species all but disappeared from some areas during the harmful algal bloom that began in early 2025.

item.title: Water storage feud heats up as frustrated Central Queensland farmers take on corporate giants
item.description: Central Queensland irrigators are demanding a "fair go" from two mega water users that are pumping from a ratepayer-owned storage without paying anything for its multimillion-dollar costs.

item.title: What we know about the two-week ceasefire deal between the US and Iran
item.description: These are the key moments that lead up to a two-week ceasefire agreement between the US and Iran, and what is on the table to permanently end the fighting.

item.title: What is financial stress and how does it affect our decisions?
item.description: If financial stress is causing distractions — for example, you're worrying about money when you should be concentrating on driving or work — there might be something deeper happening.

item.title: Shayna Jack 'lighter' as she strives for Commonwealth Games selection
item.description: Shayna Jack says her Paris Olympics campaign was fuelled by "anger and hate", but she is back to being herself — "lighter" and with more personal belief than ever.

item.title: SA farmers gamble on early winter crops after record rain, but expensive fuel and fertiliser a big concern
item.description: Some SA farmers have rolled the dice on planting winter crops weeks earlier than usual thanks to record-breaking rains. Now they hope the gamble pays off.

item.title: The far side revealed: Artemis II's lunar fly-by in pictures
item.description: A record-breaking voyage, an earthset in silence, and a total solar eclipse — astronauts on the Artemis II mission experienced sights no human has witnessed in half a century.

item.title: Bones discovered during bus stop construction work on Rottnest Island, sparking investigation
item.description: Police are investigating the origin of several human bones that were found on Rottnest Island.

item.title: Jackie O complained about Kyle Sandilands months before on-air fight, court documents allege
item.description: Court documents allege Jackie "O" Henderson was subject to "offensive and degrading" comments about her personal and dating life from co-host Kyle Sandilands in two separate incidents last year.

item.title: Pet dog survives crocodile attack at Lake Argyle in WA's remote Kimberley
item.description: A pet dog has a lucky escape after being dragged underwater by a freshwater crocodile at Lake Argyle in WA's remote Kimberley.

item.title: Queensland Rail extends planned train track closures citing protected industrial action by union workers
item.description: Train commuters across Brisbane will be impacted by an additional four days of track closures with Queensland Rail citing protected industrial action by union workers as the reason for the extension.

item.title: Sam Williamson, Mollie O'Callaghan, Kaylee McKeown, Lani Pallister, Sam Short claim wins at Australian Open swimming championships
item.description: Sam Williamson capped his comeback with victory in the 50m breaststroke just 11 months after he suffered a career-threatening injury.

item.title: US architect Rex Heuermann admits to murder of eight women in Gilgo Beach case
item.description: Rex Heuermann pleads guilty to seven murders and admits to killing another woman, solving the notorious case known as the Gilgo Beach killings.

item.title: The Australian Ballet and Bangarra Dance Theatre unite for Flora after failed referendum
item.description: Sixteen dancers from Bangarra Dance Theatre and 19 ballet dancers from the Australian Ballet have joined forces for Flora, choreographed by Bangarra artistic director Frances Rings.

item.title: How long will it take petrol prices to get back to pre-Iran war levels, if at all?
item.description: Iran's promise to reopen the Strait of Hormuz after striking a temporary agreement with the US saw the cost of oil fall sharply. But how quickly are we likely to see relief at the bowser in Australia? Analysts say it could be weeks or months.

item.title: Anthony Albanese dashes to Singapore as Australia engages in a costly, complex fuel scramble
item.description: Anthony Albanese is flying to Singapore as part of a hastily arranged regional mission to shore up crucial fuel supplies, amid warnings the global energy crisis could "sharply turn for the worse".

item.title: NT government urged to release 'secretive' review into Kumanjayi White's death
item.description: The family of Indigenous man Kumanjayi White has criticised the secrecy around the independent review into his death, which has been handed to NT Police and the DPP but not released publicly.

item.title: Farmers furious as Victorian government fast-tracks caged hen rule changes
item.description: The lives of caged hens are set to improve as Victoria implements new welfare laws, but farmers say they have not been consulted.

item.title: Public pledges sought for Justice For Her to help spark 'actions' to reduce gendered violence rates
item.description: A new platform, dubbed Justice For Her, is centred on making small, actionable pledges to help people overcome the overwhelming statistics on gendered violence, which often lead to inaction.

item.title: Severe Cyclone Maila moving again towards Far North Queensland after stalling in Solomon Sea
item.description: Cyclone Maila has woken up and is heading west, with the category four system still likely to reach Queensland's far north on Monday.

item.title: FOI reveals extent of Adelaide Writers' Week crisis, including locked doors and threats to staff
item.description: An extraordinary series of emails sheds light on the reservations that preceded, and internal fallout that followed, the decision to scrap Randa Abdel-Fattah's Writers' Week appearance.

item.title: Police investigate fatal shooting of man at Box Hill in Melbourne's east
item.description: Homicide squad detectives from Victoria Police are investigating a fatal shooting at a Box Hill apartment building.

item.title: As Jess Hull chases elusive international gold, Claudia Hollingsworth is looming over her shoulder
item.description: Olympic silver medallist Jess Hull is used to setting the benchmark in Australian middle-distance running, but Claudia Hollingsworth is coming for her 1,500-metre crown at this weekend's National Championships.

item.title: Calls to review Australian food labelling laws as farmers fight cheap imports
item.description: Farmers battling cheap imported produce want a review of food labelling laws to highlight what Australian produce is included in products.

item.title: UK man who sexually abused girl has visa reinstated over 'strong ties' to Australia
item.description: The man's permanent visa was automatically cancelled when he was jailed in 2024 for sexually abusing a nine-year-old girl, but a tribunal has upheld his appeal in part due to his "ill health".

item.title: Unprecedented wave of Israeli air strikes hit multiple areas in Lebanon’s Beirut
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: WA food charities inundated as fuel crisis sends more people to their doors
item.description: From shrinking their hampers to closing waitlists, food charities in Western Australia are adapting their services to meet a surge in demand as more people struggle with rising costs.

item.title: Melbourne Comedy Festival: Grace Jarvis on comedy as an autistic artform, chronic pain and working in a sex shop
item.description: Grace Jarvis talks about everything from her experiences with autism, ADHD, queerness, and working in a sex shop in her stand-up. Her experiences with chronic illness? Not so much.

item.title: Geraldton house vacant for more than a year after retiree renter forced to leave
item.description: After a lifetime of caring for the elderly, Linda Braam is left battling for a place to call home.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz ship traffic remains at standstill despite Iran, US and Israel ceasefire
item.description: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has remained at an effective standstill in the 24 hours since Iran conditionally lifted its blockade on the critical shipping lane, amid a fragile ceasefire with the US and Israel.

item.title: NT government in talks with Daly River residents, landowners over potential relocation of Nauiyu
item.description: Flood-affected residents from Daly River are in talks with the NT government about moving the remote Aboriginal community to higher ground, with a church offering to donate land for the township's relocation.

item.title: China's influence on the US-Iran ceasefire deal
item.description: China's Foreign Minister Wang Yi says he held 26 phone calls with his counterparts in Iran, Israel, Russia and Gulf countries before the ceasefire was reached.

item.title: JD Vance denies including Lebanon in Iran ceasefire deal
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Rain falls in Adelaide for AFL Gather Round, as interest shown for second event in NSW, Queensland
item.description: AFL fans are facing a weekend of wet weather for Gather Round as the South Australian Premier says he is "not going to rush" negotiations on the future of the event.

item.title: Trump's 'end of civilisation' threat could have consequences that outlast the war
item.description: Donald Trump has survived what may have brought down many presidents before him. But the "end of civilisation" threat, as it's become known, has exposed a vulnerability he has not faced before.

item.title: Tom Duffy to start for Brisbane Broncos, Adam Reynolds ruled out of Cowboys clash
item.description: Brisbane captain Adam Reynolds has succumbed to injury and will not play against North Queensland.

item.title: People living in vehicles forced to choose between diesel and food as fuel prices remain high
item.description: The high cost of fuel is making it even more difficult for people living in their cars to afford food, electricity and transport.

item.title: Lebanese fear war could get 'much worse' as Israel attacks amid fragile ceasefire
item.description: As a shaky ceasefire struggles to get under way, deadly Israeli strikes on Lebanon risk worsening the Middle East conflict.

item.title: What makes a relationship de facto and how can it impact a break-up?
item.description: Our "ordinary understanding" of de facto relationships may not match up with the legal definition and that can cause big surprises for people in the event of a break-up.

item.title: Iran war fuel crisis financially benefiting Russia and Vladimir Putin
item.description: Donald Trump used to love using the word "winning", but out of the wreckage of the Iran war and the world's oil supplies slowing, there is one man truly winning: Russia's Vladimir Putin.

item.title: Police investigation underway after officer pushes Indigenous woman to the ground during arrest in Broome
item.description: An internal police investigation is underway, after video footage emerged online showing a police officer pushing a woman to the ground in Western Australia's far north.

item.title: UK deployed military to deter Russian submarines from targeting undersea cables
item.description: The UK issues a warning to Moscow after revealing its military has tracked Russian submarines operating in the North Sea.

item.title: NATO chief Mark Rutte defends alliance as Trump threatens US withdrawal over Iran
item.description: Mark Rutte says European nations and other US allies are playing a vital role in supporting American military operations as he seeks to defend the alliance from Donald Trump's attacks.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: A historical orbit, emotional support animals and a tiny outback town
item.description: It's been a big week in the news. Did you catch these stories?

item.title: Do you ever need to throw out your old bath towels?
item.description: Expert tips on getting the most out of your favourite bath towels and when it might be time to replace them.

item.title: How the PM's Singapore visit could help Australia's fuel supply
item.description: Experts say visits like Anthony Albanese's trip to Singapore are important for securing Australia's energy supplies. As one observer puts it: "When you don't have the stock, you need friends."

item.title: TelstraSuper closes Direct Access investment product without properly notifying members
item.description: A superannuation investment option spruiked as offering "choice and flexibility" has been shut down without proper notification, leaving members less than two months to sell all their assets.

item.title: The NDIS's 'social licence' isn't just at risk of eroding – it's close to collapse
item.description: The NDIS's reputation isn't just at risk of coming under pressure, it's well and truly crumbling, write Nas Campanella and Evan Young.

item.title: Taylor and Canavan are chalk and cheese – and that's a problem for Taylor
item.description: If you want a basic measure of the difference between Liberal leader Angus Taylor and Nationals leader Matt Canavan, compare their reactions to US President Donald Trump's extraordinary threat this week that "a whole civilisation will die tonight".

item.title: Racism erupts after Melbourne announces Little India plans, but some Indians say it will build connection
item.description: Social media posts about the announcement of a Little India precinct in Melbourne are flooded with racist comments, leaving some Indians with mixed feelings about the plan.

item.title: Cyclone Maila weakens to category two as it slowly heads to Queensland
item.description: Cyclone Maila has been downgraded to a category two system, pushing back the expected time it will make landfall to Tuesday.

item.title: Broken Hill sprinter Olufemi Komolafe's journey to 2026 Stawell Gift win
item.description: Olufemi Komolafe is Broken Hill's first winner of the Stawell Gift since 1949. The 21-year-old sprinter and med student took out the men’s final at the iconic 2026 foot race in Western Victoria over the Easter long weekend.

item.title: Israel's backflip on Lebanon may be posturing, but it's keeping the fragile peace. What you need to know on Iran today
item.description: The need to ensure the Iran ceasefire holds until the first round of peace talks are held has put Israel under pressure.

item.title: NSW drug diversion scheme disproportionately sends Aboriginal people to court, report says
item.description: A BOSCAR report finds NSW Police disproportionately used "discretion" to reject eligible low-level drug offenders from a diversion program — especially Aboriginal people.

item.title: Tiwi Island birder hopes rare photos of endemic owl encourage conservation
item.description: A traditional owner on a Northern Australian island has captured rare photos of an "enchanting" masked owl, adding to the limited knowledge base surrounding the endemic and culturally significant bird.

item.title: Australian role in Hormuz depends on permanent ceasefire, Marles says — as it happened
item.description: Australia has been involved in talks about assisting the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz but any action can only take place if a ceasefire becomes permanent, Richard Marles says.

item.title: Study finds Tasmanian native forest logging increases potential for more severe bushfires
item.description: Scientists have studied satellite images of a Tasmanian bushfire and found regrowth from extensive logging and recent bushfires has "absolutely" increased the risk of more severe bushfires.

item.title: ASX 200 and Aussie dollar down, Brent crude and WTI oil up — as it happened
item.description: The Australian market has finished the trading week on a disappointing note, dropping slightly, despite US stocks rising overnight. Look back on our business blog.

item.title: Russian President Vladimir Putin declares ceasefire in Ukraine for Orthodox Easter
item.description: The Russian president declared the ceasefire following Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy proposing one earlier this week.

item.title: Cheat Sheet: Charting Euphoria's long and messy road to season three
item.description: Sam Levinson's controversial teen series has had to overcome four years of actor deaths, character departures and on-set tension to make it to season three.

item.title: Aussie rock band DMA'S on performing at the AFL Gather Round
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Inside the secret Pakistan talks that brokered the fragile Iran war ceasefire
item.description: For a few volatile hours, the war in the Middle East appeared to be tipping past the point of no return — until a last-minute push of diplomacy pulled it back.

item.title: Hills Shire Mayor leads calls to simplify governance of Hawkesbury River
item.description: The Hawkesbury-Nepean River flows through eight NSW council areas and is looked after by 13 agencies, which one mayor says leads to confusion about who is in charge of what.

item.title: Chimpanzees made "territorial patrols'
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Chimpanzee's in Uganda's Kibale National Park fight
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Chimpanzees in Uganda's Kibale National Park wage lethal 'civil war'
item.description: A decades-long study reveals hundreds of chimpanzees waging a violent and deadly conflict against their former friends in their remote forest home.

item.title: Pilbara residents launch petition after warning system confusion during cyclone
item.description: Residents in the Pilbara raise concerns over the state's warning system for cyclones following ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle last month.

item.title: Why the Iran war fuel crisis will get worse before it gets better
item.description: The official pledges of the last few days may have placated markets at first, but they have become little more than bargaining chips ahead of this weekend's negotiations between the US and Iran.

item.title: Cyclone Maila batters Solomon Islands as it moves through PNG towards Far North Queensland
item.description: Roads and buildings have been destroyed across multiple Pacific islands as slow-moving Tropical Cyclone Maila heads towards Papua New Guinea, with the eye of the storm set to cross over two remote islands early tomorrow.

item.title: Australia vs Great Britain Billie Jean King Cup: Talia Gibson and Kimberly Birrell lose singles
item.description: Australia's quest to win a place in the Billie Jean King Cup Finals is off to the worst possible start after losses in both opening-day singles matches.

item.title: What to watch and listen to this weekend
item.description: An old favourite, new Courtney Barnett and a tasty Korean vegetarian stew.

item.title: Artemis II crew about to make splash landing on Earth after journey around Moon
item.description: The Orion space capsule will enter the Earth's atmosphere as a burning fireball, travelling at about 40,000 km/h, splashing down off the coast of southern California. Here's a breakdown of how the spacecraft copes with high temperatures, slows down, and what happens as it splashes down.

item.title: Afrika Bambaataa, rap icon accused of sexual abuse, dead at 67
item.description: The Universal Zulu Nation founder was a beloved hip hop icon before sexual abuse and trafficking allegations surfaced in the 2010s.

item.title: Blayze James Noble charged with murder of Christine Joyce Diane Marshall Hunter
item.description: A Sunshine Coast man has been charged with murdering his girlfriend in the Northern Territory. Ahead of his appearance in court, her sister has spoken of her family's heartbreak.

item.title: Man pleads guilty to dealing with millions in cash from illegal tobacco sales
item.description: Malek Alhmedai's hire car had $4 million stashed inside, with the money linked to illegal tobacco sales.

item.title: Australia's economy is underpinned by housing, which is why housing won't get cheaper, super boss warns
item.description: AustralianSuper boss says housing prices unlikely to fall much, because major banks can't afford them too

item.title: Commuters trapped in South Eastern Freeway gridlock blast lack of signage
item.description: Commuters stuck queuing for up to four hours following a truck crash on the South Eastern Freeway on Thursday blast the transport department over a lack of signs warning them of the road closure ahead.

item.title: Anthony Albanese and Singapore's prime minister talk trade, fuel in bilateral meeting
item.description: Australia and Singapore have agreed to make "maximum efforts" to meet each other's fuel and gas needs as the crisis in the Middle East continues, with the prime minister securing an encouraging but not legally binding deal.

item.title: Taiwan's opposition leader Cheng Li-wun has met Xi Jinping in China for the first time
item.description: Taiwan's opposition leader has done something for the first time in a decade, and experts say it could shape relations between Beijing, Taipei and Washington.

item.title: Cowboys claim thrilling Queensland derby victory with last-minute try
item.description: The North Queensland Cowboys have won a thriller at Lang Park, beating the Brisbane Broncos 35-31. Earlier, the Dragons lost their sixth-straight to start the 2026 NRL season.

item.title: Dunsborough Primary School left scrambling to pay $130k water bill
item.description: Dunsborough Primary School is racking up massive water bills in comparison to nearby schools, to the detriment of student support and repairs to facilities.

item.title: Dockers launch late fightback to pip Magpies in frantic finish
item.description: Fremantle wins its fourth match in a row after surviving a tense final few minutes against Collingwood at Adelaide Oval.

item.title: Australian War Memorial display on Ben Roberts-Smith altered as refurbished sections unveiled
item.description: As the Australian War Memorial unveils some of its newest elements as part of a $500 million redevelopment, director Matt Anderson confirms it has altered a display dedicated to Ben Roberts-Smith in the wake of his arrest for alleged war crimes.

item.title: Court releases vision of attack on off-duty officer
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: St Kilda forward Lance Collard guilty over using homophobic slur
item.description: St Kilda forward Lance Collard has been found guilty of using a homophobic slur against an opponent, leaving his AFL career hanging in the balance.

item.title: US Vice-President JD Vance heads to Pakistan for Iran peace talks
item.description: The centre of Islamabad has been locked down as US delegates, including JD Vance, travel to Pakistan for peace talks with Iran.

item.title: UK charity Sentebale sues Prince Harry for defamation
item.description: Prince Harry co-founded Sentebale 20 years ago in honour of his late mother, Princess Diana, but now the organisation is suing him for defamation.

item.title: Albanese's petro-diplomacy can't overcome the pain of what may lie ahead
item.description: This is not over. The hard stuff is only just beginning. And it's going to get bumpy, testing the nation's resilience and its political leadership.

item.title: Debra Adelaide reflects on friendship with Gabrielle Carey in When I Am Sixty-Four
item.description: Debra Adelaide became friends with Gabrielle Carey when they were 12. After Carey died by suicide five decades later, Adelaide tried to address her unanswered questions in a novel.

item.title: Cosplay is rising in popularity. What are the rules for dressing up?
item.description: Cosplayers say their hobby has had an astronomical rise in popularity over the past decade. While the subculture promotes "no limits" creativity, there are still strict rules.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: This week we're testing your knowledge of a classic Aussie poem, oyster recipes and Itchy and Scratchy
item.description: Every question has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

item.title: Trump, Netanyahu and how war in Iran is reshaping US politics
item.description: Extraordinary reporting reveals how Israel made the case for regime change in Iran — and how the fallout is driving a profound shift in American opinion, amid deep uncertainty about what the war has achieved.

item.title: Israel admits it did not expect Iran would close the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: How Australia is navigating an oil market in crisis
item.description: Australia is stitching together a steady supply of fuel through a patchwork of relationships. But how long can that last?

item.title: Philippine music takes global stage as P-pop groups secure gigs at major US festivals
item.description: As one of the world's largest music festivals gets underway this weekend, a pop group from the Philippines will make history.

item.title: Iran uses sea mine threat to 'maintain chokehold' over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran claims to have littered a central shipping passage in the Strait of Hormuz with sea mines, in a move analysts say could be a "coercive threat" to maintain its grip on the crucial waterway.

item.title: Live: Trump theatens Iran with warships ahead of 'make-or-break' talks
item.description: US Vice-President JD Vance is due to arrive in Pakistan as both sides level accusations of ceasefire breaches. Follow live.

item.title: War hero to accused war criminal: Inside Ben Roberts-Smith's prosecution
item.description: Once celebrated as Australia's most decorated living war hero, the looming prosecution of Ben Roberts-Smith is shaping up to be the next trial of the century.

item.title: Israel's ambassador concedes Iran's chokehold on Strait of Hormuz was not expected before war
item.description: Israeli ambassador Hillel Newman admits his country was surprised by the scope of Iran's response to the Middle East war and that it did not expect Gulf states to be targeted in retaliation strikes.

item.title: Israel admits it did not expect Iran would close the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Jess Hull crashes out and Claudia Hollingsworth disqualified in dramatic women's 1,500m final
item.description: Jess Hull had been on track to claim a fourth-straight 1,500m national title before being clipped by Claudia Hollingsworth, bringing her reign crashing down.

item.title: Expansion on cards at Eromanga refinery as Queensland looks to produce more oil
item.description: The Inland Oil Refinery produces specialised underground mining fuel, heating oils and other speciality solvents, and supplies a small amount of on-road diesel for the local community

item.title: Milk prices may need to increase as dairy farmers hit hard by fuel and fertiliser costs
item.description: Dairy farmers across Australia are battling crippling fuel and fertiliser costs. The industry is now calling for a 30-cent-per-litre rise in milk prices on supermarket shelves.

item.title: Greyhound trainers frustrated over 'lack of accountability' as safety issues continue at Ipswich facility
item.description: Ipswich facility The Q was promised to be "as safe for dogs and trainers as possible", but several dogs died during its trial period and events have been cancelled due to unsafe conditions.

item.title: Alex de Minaur suffers painful Monte Carlo quarterfinal loss to Monaco's Valentin Vacherot
item.description: Alex de Minaur will slip from his career-high ranking after missing a chance to emulate his semifinal run at the Monte Carlo Masters, beaten in three sets by Monaco's Valentin Vacherot.

item.title: Renewed push for completion of dumped Victorian Murray Basin freight rail project
item.description: North-west Victorian farmers are pushing for the completion of the Murray Basin Rail Project, more than seven years after it was originally slated to be finished.

item.title: Long-range goals and their impact on the 2026 AFL season
item.description: In a league where inside-50s are so often king, teams with prowess from outside the arc are making a major impact to start the 2026 AFL season.

item.title: Locals fear Jamisontown church will be bulldozed to build new homes
item.description: Residents on a suburban street in Western Sydney fear 130 years of history will be erased if a 19th century church is bulldozed to build new homes.

item.title: More modern buildings being added to SA heritage list, as owners raise concerns
item.description: More modern buildings are being added to South Australia's state heritage list, especially churches, but some owners object to their properties being preserved, worried that it will add cost and complexity to their plans.

item.title: Live: NASA's Artemis II astronauts prepare for splashdown
item.description: After orbiting the Moon, four astronauts on board the Orion capsule are preparing to return to Earth.

item.title: Police appeal for information about mysterious shooting death of Richard Wills at Ouyen
item.description: Richard Wills was shot and buried in a shallow grave at his rural property in Victoria's north-west earlier this week.

item.title: British man who avoided deportation after child sex offence could face fresh visa review
item.description: A British child sex offender who avoided deportation is facing a possible visa review, after an ABC investigation.

item.title: Behind the scenes at Armidale's Natural History Museum.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Armidale museum freezes dozens of koalas for taxidermy and research
item.description: Most people usually drive past animals killed on the road, but in Armidale, they stop, bag them up, and drop them off at the local museum.

item.title: Rotting sperm whale carcass forces relocation of Tasmania's largest open water swim
item.description: A 40-tonne sperm whale carcass has forced the relocation of Tasmania's biggest open water swim. Organisers of the event had hoped it would be moved, but the state's environment department says that was not feasible.

item.title: Tamworth teen Max Lye is no chicken in the egg business
item.description: While many teenagers spend their spare time online gaming or playing sport, 14-year-old Max Lye hangs out with a bunch of chooks and dreams of owning his own egg farm.

item.title: Plans to move residents off Cocos (Keeling) Islands walked back after backlash
item.description: After seething criticism from residents, the Commonwealth has released its final plan to protect the Cocos (Keeling) Islands from rising sea levels.
